title: helianthemum globulariifolium var. minor
intro: Helianthemum globulariifolium var. minor, also known as Rock Rose, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pink or white flowers and small, oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for colds, coughs, and skin conditions. Rock Rose can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to treat wounds, burns, and skin infections.
